name: Ramaco Resources, Inc.
business: Ramaco Resources is a dual platform critical mineral company that sells high-quality, low-cost metallurgical coal to domestic and international steel producers and coke plants, and is developing rare earth element and critical mineral operations. The company's metallurgical coal is sold to North American blast furnace steel mills and coke plants as well as international customers in Europe, South America, Asia, and Africa. The rare earth and critical mineral segment is not yet in commercial production.
sellsTo: North American integrated steel mills and coke plants
International customers in Europe, South America, Asia, and Africa

exposures.sentence: Metallurgical coal, also known as met coal or coking coal, is a key component of the blast furnace steelmaking process and, therefore, demand for such coal is highly correlated with conditions in the steel industry.
The Company's Brook Mine is believed to host a rich and unconventional deposit of rare earth elements and critical minerals embedded in coal seams and carbonaceous clays and shales.

In addition, the deposit includes high-value critical minerals such as gallium (Ga), germanium (Ge), and scandium (Sc), which are used in semiconductors, aerospace alloys, and advanced communication technologies.

In addition, the deposit includes high-value critical minerals such as gallium (Ga), germanium (Ge), and scandium (Sc), which are used in semiconductors, aerospace alloys, and advanced communication technologies.

Demand for rare earth elements and critical minerals may be impacted by demand for downstream products such as hybrid and electric vehicles, wind turbines, robotics, medical equipment, military equipment and other high-growth, advanced motion technologies

catalysts.event: Ribbon cutting and groundbreaking at Brook Mine
catalysts.sentence: We had a ribbon cutting and groundbreaking at our carbon ore and rare earth mine, the Brook Mine, on July 11, 2025.

catalysts.event: Fluor issued Preliminary Economic Assessment for Brook Mine
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, the Fluor Corporation (Fluor) issued a Preliminary Economic Assessment (the PEA) in which it concluded that the Brook Mine was both technically and economically viable based on its findings and the product pricing information provided by the Company.

catalysts.event: Weir issued new Technical Report Summary for Brook Mine
catalysts.sentence: On September 17, 2025, we received a new geological TRS from Weir, updated from the March 2025 study.

In November 2025, the Company acquired what it refers to as the Russell County property which are contiguous with the Knox Creek complex and which is expected to significantly increase the reserves and resources at Knox Creek.

catalysts.event: Third Amended and Restated Credit and Security Agreement entered
catalysts.sentence: On December 30, 2025, the Company entered into a Third Amended and Restated Credit and Security Agreement, which includes KeyBank National Association and multiple lending parties, in order to, among other things, extend the maturity date and increase the size of the facility.
The subsequent more detailed pre-feasibility study, also being prepared by Hatch, is now expected for completion by late 2026.
